stories.title: Sinner Extends Streak to 31, Advances to Roland Garros R3

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Sinner takes down Cerundolo in straight sets at Roland Garros to reach the third round. Win streak: 31. The career Grand Slam is four rounds away. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #Sinner #FrenchOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: 31 wins in a row. No. 1 seed. The only major missing is Roland Garros. Sinner is through to the third round and the career Grand Slam is still on. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #Sinner #FrenchOpen

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Jannik Sinner is marching through Roland Garros like a man on a mission — and he took another step toward tennis history on Thursday, beating Argentine Juan Manuel Cerundolo in straight sets to advance to the third round.

The win extends Sinner's winning streak to 31 consecutive matches, one of the longest runs in the modern ATP era. More importantly for the history books, it keeps alive his bid for the career Grand Slam. Sinner has won the Australian Open twice (2024 and 2025), the US Open in 2024, and Wimbledon in 2025. The French Open is the only major that has escaped him — and he's now four rounds from changing that.

Cerundolo, an Argentine clay-court specialist, gave Sinner few problems. The World No. 1 was in command throughout, using his trademark heavy groundstrokes and impeccable movement to control rallies. The match on Court Philippe-Chatrier was as one-sided as the score suggests.

Four wins to go. Can Sinner complete the career Grand Slam?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Day 5: Sinner def. Cerundolo in straight sets. Win streak: 31. Career Grand Slam bid very much alive at Roland Garros.

article.headline: Sinner Extends Win Streak to 31, Advances to Roland Garros Third Round

stories.title: Rybakina Shock Exit — Starodubtseva Stuns No. 2 Seed, Women's Draw Blown Open

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: No. 2 seed Elena Rybakina is out of Roland Garros. Yuliia Starodubtseva won 3-6, 6-1, 7-6(10-4) yesterday for her first career top-10 victory. The women's draw is wide open. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #Rybakina #WTA

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

The biggest upset of Roland Garros 2026 landed on Wednesday — and it wasn't even the headline match.

Ukrainian Yuliia Starodubtseva eliminated No. 2 seed Elena Rybakina 3-6, 6-1, 7-6(10-4) in two hours and 28 minutes on Court Simonne-Mathieu. For Starodubtseva, it was the biggest win of her career — her first victory over a top-10 opponent after six previous defeats.

She won the second set 6-1 after surrendering the first 3-6, then broke early in the decider to lead 3-0. It looked like a rout was coming. Instead, Rybakina recovered to 4-4 and forced a nerve-shredding finish. The match tiebreak, however, belonged entirely to Starodubtseva. She won it 10-4.

The Kazakhstani finished with 71 unforced errors against just 23 winners. That ratio — more than three errors for every winner — is simply not survivable tennis at this level. The women's bottom half was projected to produce a Rybakina vs. Gauff semifinal. That narrative is over now.

Who do you think benefits most from Rybakina's exit?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

Biggest upset of Roland Garros 2026: No. 2 seed Elena Rybakina is out. Yuliia Starodubtseva won 3-6, 6-1, 7-6(10-4) for her first career top-10 win. The women's draw just changed.

article.headline: Starodubtseva Stuns No. 2 Seed Rybakina in Roland Garros Shock Exit

stories.title: Osaka Reaches Roland Garros R3 for First Time Since 2019

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Naomi Osaka is in the Roland Garros third round for the first time since 2019. She beat Donna Vekic 7-6(1), 6-4 today. Next: 18-year-old Iva Jovic. The comeback rolls on. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #Osaka #FrenchOpen

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

Naomi Osaka has reached the Roland Garros third round for the first time since 2019 — and it might just be the moment her comeback truly arrives on the biggest clay stage in tennis.

Osaka, the No. 16 seed, defeated Croatian Donna Vekic 7-6(1), 6-4 on Thursday in a controlled performance that showed the four-time Grand Slam champion's growing confidence on a surface that has traditionally been difficult for her. She won the match tiebreak 7-1, a display of focus that erased any doubt about the result.

Just 16 months ago, Osaka was ranked No. 135 in the world, returning to the tour after maternity leave with a ranking and a reputation to rebuild. Since then, she has won titles, climbed back to the top 20, and now stands in the third round of Roland Garros.

Her next match is against 18-year-old American Iva Jovic, who didn't give Osaka's compatriot Emma Navarro so much as a look on Thursday, winning 6-0, 6-3. Jovic is fearless and full of upside.

What's your take — can Osaka make a real run at Roland Garros this year?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

Naomi Osaka: Roland Garros third round for the first time since 2019. She beat Donna Vekic 7-6(1), 6-4. Next: Iva Jovic. The comeback keeps going.

article.headline: Osaka Reaches Roland Garros Third Round for First Time Since 2019

stories.title: Day 4 Recap — Djokovic Survives, Fonseca Sets Dream Clash, Swiatek's Evert Record

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: From Wednesday at Roland Garros: Djokovic survives Royer in four sets (record 21st straight R3). Fonseca rallied from 2 sets down to face his idol Djokovic next. Swiatek tied Evert's Open Era RG record. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 9:00 PM ET

Wednesday at Roland Garros delivered four compelling storylines — a record, a comeback for the ages, an injured exit, and a piece of history matched.

Novak Djokovic defeated French wildcard Valentin Royer 6-3, 6-2, 6-7(7), 6-3 to reach the third round for the 21st consecutive year. Royer took the third set in a tiebreak and saved three match points before Djokovic closed it out. Twenty-one consecutive Roland Garros third rounds. That's a number that puts the longevity of Djokovic's clay career into clear perspective.

Meanwhile, 19-year-old Joao Fonseca staged one of the great Roland Garros comebacks. He trailed Croatian Dino Prizmic by two sets before winning the last three 6-3, 6-1, 6-2 to prevail 3-6, 4-6, 6-3, 6-1, 6-2. He became just the third teenager this century to overcome a two-set deficit at Roland Garros — after Roger Federer in 2001 and Thanasi Kokkinakis in 2015. His reward: a third-round clash with Novak Djokovic, the player Fonseca has called his idol and the greatest of all time.

Alexander Zverev dispatched Czech Tomas Machac 6-4, 6-2, 6-2 in one hour and 48 minutes after Machac suffered a lower body injury. And Iga Swiatek defeated Sara Bejlek 6-2, 6-3, equaling Chris Evert's Open Era record of 42 wins from 45 main draw matches at Roland Garros.

Which storyline caught your eye most from Wednesday's action?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 9:00 PM ET

Day 4 at Roland Garros: Djokovic's record 21st straight R3. Fonseca's epic five-set comeback to face his idol next. Swiatek ties Chris Evert's Open Era record at this tournament. Quite a Wednesday.

article.headline: Roland Garros Day 4 Recap: Djokovic's Record, Fonseca's Comeback, Swiatek's History

stories.title: Day 6 Preview — Djokovic vs Fonseca Headlines Friday

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Friday at Roland Garros: Djokovic (39) vs Fonseca (19). The legend vs the kid who calls him the greatest of all time. Third round starts. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #Djokovic #FrenchOpen

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 6:00 PM ET

Roland Garros has its best matchup of the tournament lined up for Friday — and it didn't even exist until a 19-year-old Brazilian refused to quit on Wednesday.

Joao Fonseca will step onto court to face Novak Djokovic in the third round. Fonseca has spoken publicly about Djokovic being his idol, the player who made him fall in love with tennis, the greatest of all time. Now he gets to play him at Roland Garros. Djokovic is 39, determined to chase a 25th Grand Slam title. Fonseca is 19, riding the momentum of a five-set comeback, and playing without fear.

Meanwhile, the women's draw enters third-round action without No. 2 seed Elena Rybakina, who was stunned by Yuliia Starodubtseva on Wednesday. Defending champion Coco Gauff and No. 1 seed Aryna Sabalenka are the standard-bearers. And lurking for four-time champion Iga Swiatek: Jelena Ostapenko, who leads their all-time head-to-head 6-0.

What are you most looking forward to at Roland Garros on Friday?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 6:00 PM ET

Friday at Roland Garros: Djokovic (39) vs Fonseca (19). The idol vs the kid. Third round begins. This is the matchup of the tournament.

article.headline: Roland Garros Day 6 Preview: Djokovic vs Fonseca Headlines a Loaded Friday
